examples.title: Inheritance Example
examples.description: An example of inheritance in C++.
examples.code: class Parent { public: void print() { cout << "Parent" << endl; } }; class Child : public Parent { public: void print() { cout << "Child" << endl; } }; int main() { Child c; c.print(); return 0; }
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of inheritance in C++.

examples.title: Polymorphism Example
examples.description: An example of polymorphism in C++.
examples.code: class Shape { public: virtual void draw() = 0; }; class Circle : public Shape { public: void draw() { cout << "Circle" << endl; } }; class Rectangle : public Shape { public: void draw() { cout << "Rectangle" << endl; } }; int main() { Shape* s = new Circle(); s->draw(); s = new Rectangle(); s->draw(); return 0; }
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of polymorphism in C++.

examples.title: Encapsulation Example
examples.description: An example of encapsulation in C++.
examples.code: class BankAccount { private: int balance; public: void deposit(int amount) { balance += amount; } void withdraw(int amount) { balance -= amount; } int getBalance() { return balance; } }; int main() { BankAccount ba; ba.deposit(100); ba.withdraw(50); cout << ba.getBalance() << endl; return 0; }
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of encapsulation in C++.

examples.title: Abstraction Example
examples.description: An example of abstraction in C++.
examples.code: class Vehicle { public: virtual void drive() = 0; }; class Car : public Vehicle { public: void drive() { cout << "Car" << endl; } }; class Truck : public Vehicle { public: void drive() { cout << "Truck" << endl; } }; int main() { Vehicle* v = new Car(); v->drive(); v = new Truck(); v->drive(); return 0; }
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of abstraction in C++.

examples.title: Composition Example
examples.description: An example of composition in C++.
examples.code: class Engine { public: void start() { cout << "Engine started" << endl; } }; class Car { private: Engine e; public: void start() { e.start(); } }; int main() { Car c; c.start(); return 0; }
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of composition in C++.
